description: Binary exploitation precondition analysis. Wickets describe memory protection absence, exploitation path constructibility, and offensive capability presence. Tri-state physics: REALIZED = exploitable/offensive condition confirmed, BLOCKED = protection prevents exploitation, UNKNOWN = not yet measured.

BA-01.label: nx_disabled
BA-01.description: NX (non-executable stack/heap) not set. Stack memory is executable — shellcode injection viable without ROP.
BA-01.evidence_hint: checksec --file=<binary> | grep NX. Evidence rank 3 (config/binary attributes).

BA-02.label: aslr_disabled_or_weak
BA-02.description: PIE disabled or ASLR weak. Load address is predictable — absolute addressing in payload viable.
BA-02.evidence_hint: checksec --file=<binary> | grep PIE; cat /proc/sys/kernel/randomize_va_space. Evidence rank 3.

BA-03.label: no_stack_canary
BA-03.description: Stack canary not present. Stack overflow reaches return address without detection.

BA-04.label: dangerous_function_imported
BA-04.description: Binary calls dangerous libc function: strcpy, strcat, gets, sprintf, vsprintf, scanf, system, popen, exec.

BA-05.label: controlled_input_reaches_dangerous_call
BA-05.description: Attacker-controlled data flows to a dangerous function. Overflow path confirmed by ltrace or static analysis.
BA-05.evidence_hint: ltrace -e 'strcpy+gets+sprintf+system' ./<binary> < input; radare2 data flow. Evidence rank 2.

BA-06.label: exploit_chain_constructible
BA-06.description: Sufficient ROP gadgets exist or direct EIP/RIP control demonstrated via cyclic pattern.
BA-06.evidence_hint: ROPgadget --binary <binary> --rop; pwndbg cyclic + offset. Evidence rank 2.

BA-07.label: capability_identified
BA-07.description: capa matched ≥ 1 named capability rule against the binary. Indicates the binary implements identifiable techniques such as code injection, credential harvesting, process enumeration, or C2 communication patterns.
BA-07.evidence_hint: capa --json <binary> | python -m json.tool — check rules[*].matches. Evidence rank 2.

BA-08.label: attck_technique_confirmed
BA-08.description: capa matched ≥ 1 ATT&CK technique ID in the binary. Confirms the binary implements specific MITRE ATT&CK tradecraft (e.g. T1055 Process Injection, T1027 Obfuscation, T1059 Scripting).

BA-09.label: symbolic_vuln_path_confirmed
BA-09.description: angr symbolic execution found a feasible execution path from program entry to a dangerous function with symbolic (unconstrained) stdin input. Provides stronger evidence than import analysis alone that the path is reachable.

BA-10.label: runtime_hook_confirmed
BA-10.description: Frida dynamic instrumentation intercepted ≥ 1 dangerous function call during live execution of the binary. Argument values and call context are available. Highest-fidelity runtime evidence — requires authorized execution.
BA-10.evidence_hint: frida: Interceptor.attach(dangerous_sym, {onEnter}) — see adapter.frida_trace. Evidence rank 1. Requires authorized=True.

binary_stack_overflow_v1.description: Stack buffer overflow → code execution. Requires no NX, no canary, dangerous function reachable, controlled input, gadgets.

binary_rop_chain_v1.description: ROP chain bypasses NX → code execution. Canary absent, dangerous function reachable, controlled input, gadgets.

binary_format_string_v1.description: Format string vulnerability → arbitrary read/write. Dangerous function (printf family) reachable with controlled format arg.

binary_offensive_capability_v1.description: Binary implements identifiable offensive tradecraft confirmed by capa capability rules and ATT&CK technique mapping. Does not require exploitability — binary itself is a threat actor tool.

binary_symbolic_confirmed_v1.description: Symbolic execution (angr) confirms a feasible path from program input to dangerous function. Combined with no stack canary and dangerous imports, constitutes high-confidence pre-exploitation assessment without live execution.

binary_runtime_confirmed_v1.description: Live Frida instrumentation intercepted dangerous function calls with inspectable arguments. Provides runtime ground truth for exploit path confirmation. Requires authorized execution of the binary.
